steps.title: Spot the relationship
steps.content: Sentence 1 makes a general claim: KDKO "does much to serve the local Dakota-speaking community." Sentence 2 gives concrete examples of how: it broadcasts local news, music, and Dakota-language programming. The blank must signal that Sentence 2 zooms in on Sentence 1 with specifics.

steps.title: Eliminate and confirm
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Instead,’: signals a replacement or correction — but Sentence 2 is not replacing Sentence 1 with something different; it supports it. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Specifically,’: signals that Sentence 2 provides concrete details that elaborate on the general claim in Sentence 1. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘As a result,’: signals cause-and-effect — the broadcasts are not an outcome of the general claim; they illustrate it. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Nevertheless,’: signals contrast despite an obstacle — Sentence 2 is fully consistent with Sentence 1, not contrary to it. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
